segments.title: How to use AI for writing?
top5Tips: Specify role and audience – AI matches tone and length better.
State what you want (e.g. “3 paragraphs, 2–3 sentences each”) – clear format gives better results.
Add 1–2 style examples – AI will mirror the style.
Review the first draft and ask “shorten” or “change tone” – iterate.
Keep one language in the prompt – mixing languages lowers quality.
segments.systemPrompt: You are an experienced writer. You write clearly, concisely, and for the specified audience. If information is missing – ask one concrete question.
segments.motivationWish: Good prompts are not long text but clear steps. Start with one precise request and improve. Good luck! — Prompt Anatomy

segments.title: How to use AI for practical advice?
top5Tips: Define the situation in one sentence – what’s happening and what decision is needed.
Specify if you want a list, plan, or short answer – format helps.
Ask “suggest 3 options” – comparison leads to better choices.
Add constraints (time, resources) – advice becomes actionable.
After the advice ask “what step to take first” – action starts right away.
segments.systemPrompt: You are a practical consultant. You give short, action-oriented advice. If needed – ask one follow-up question before suggesting.
segments.motivationWish: Practice starts with one clear question. Every better prompt gets you closer to the solution. — Prompt Anatomy

segments.title: How to use AI for information search?
top5Tips: Phrase the question concretely – not “explain” but “explain X in 3 sentences”.
Specify if you need sources – some tools cite them.
Check facts critically – AI can be wrong; verify important claims across sources.
Use “summarize this article” – better than pasting full text.
If the answer is too vague – ask for “two examples” or “one source”.
segments.systemPrompt: You are a research assistant. You ground answers in sources. If you don’t know – say “I don’t know” and suggest where to check.
segments.motivationWish: Information search with AI works better when you know what you’re looking for. A precise query is half the work. — Prompt Anatomy

segments.title: How to use AI for technical help?
top5Tips: Paste the error message and briefly describe what you’re doing – context solves it.
Specify language, tool, and version – answers become more accurate.
Ask for “explain step by step” or “give corrected code” – format saves time.
If the fix doesn’t work – ask for an alternative or “debug” steps.
Keep secrets safe – don’t put passwords or API keys in prompts.
segments.systemPrompt: You are a technical assistant. You give clear steps and code examples. When needed – suggest alternatives. You never ask for secrets.
segments.motivationWish: Technical help with AI works best when the problem and context are clear. From a small bug to automation – step by step. — Prompt Anatomy

segments.title: How to use AI for multimedia?
top5Tips: Describe the image concretely: subject, action, style, mood.
Specify proportions (e.g. 16:9, 1:1) – for different channels.
Add “what to avoid” (negative prompt) – fewer bad results.
For video and audio – short script or tempo / style notes.
Respect usage rights – use platforms with clear licensing.
segments.systemPrompt: You are a visual and audio content assistant. You create from clear descriptions and style notes. You suggest proportions and usage limits.
segments.motivationWish: Multimedia with AI opens up through clear descriptions and a few tries. Start with one image or short audio – and improve. — Prompt Anatomy

segments.title: How to start using AI? (Other / not sure)
top5Tips: Start with one daily task – e.g. one email or one summary.
Pick one tool and try 3 different prompts – compare results.
Note what worked and what didn’t – your personal prompt library.
Don’t ask abstract questions – give a concrete task (“write X”, “summarize Y”).
If you don’t like the answer – ask for “shorter”, “simpler”, or “different tone”.
segments.systemPrompt: You are a friendly assistant for beginners. You explain simply and give one concrete step. You encourage trying and treat mistakes as learning.
segments.motivationWish: Every expert started with a first prompt. Start today – one small task is already a winning step. — Prompt Anatomy

segments.title: How to use AI for self-expression and creativity?
top5Tips: Describe your idea or thought freely – AI can expand or structure it.
Specify tone (personal, inspired, philosophical) – the answer will match.
Ask for “5 variants” or “3 different perspectives” – more options.
Use “imagine that…” – role prompt opens creative thinking.
Keep personal notes private – don’t put secret thoughts in shared models.
segments.systemPrompt: You are a creative partner. You help express thoughts and generate ideas without pressure. You respect personal boundaries and suggest, not command.
segments.motivationWish: Self-expression with AI is not replacement but addition. Your thoughts stay yours; AI helps refine and express them. Good luck creating! — Prompt Anatomy
